pages.title: PocketMC Desktop — Overview
pages.purpose: High-level introduction to PocketMC Desktop: what it is, who it is for, key capabilities, technology stack, and how the major subsystems relate to each other. Links to all child sections for deeper reading.

pages.title: Getting Started & First-Run Setup
pages.purpose: Covers installation via Velopack, the first-launch wizard (root directory selection, Java provisioning), and the ShellStartupCoordinator flow that gates the user into the dashboard.

pages.title: Build, Packaging & CI/CD
pages.purpose: Documents the GitHub Actions production-build workflow, Velopack packaging, version comparison logic, and how delta updates are generated and distributed.

pages.title: Application Architecture
pages.purpose: Overview of the .NET 8 WPF application structure: the Generic Host composition root, MVVM pattern, dependency injection modules, navigation system, and core infrastructure abstractions. Links to child pages for each major layer.

pages.title: Dependency Injection & Service Registration
pages.purpose: Explains how AddCoreInfrastructure, AddInstanceManagement, AddTunneling, AddMarketplace, and AddPresentation extension methods compose the service container, and which services are singletons vs transient.

pages.title: MVVM Core & Navigation
pages.purpose: Covers ViewModelBase, RelayCommand, AsyncRelayCommand, IAppNavigationService, AppNavigationService, ControlledNavigationStack, and how pages are resolved and cached in the shell.

pages.title: Shell & Main Window
pages.purpose: Details the MainWindow shell host, navigation locking, title bar context, TrayIconViewModel, system tray lifecycle, update banner, and the ShellViewModel update pipeline.

pages.title: Infrastructure Services
pages.purpose: Documents cross-cutting infrastructure: PhysicalFileSystem, WpfDialogService, WpfAppDispatcher, WindowsToastNotificationService, DataProtector, JobObject, UwpLoopbackHelper, and WallpaperMicaService.

pages.title: Instance Management
pages.purpose: Overview of how Minecraft server instances are created, stored, configured, and deleted. Links to child pages covering the full lifecycle from creation through runtime to backup.

pages.title: Instance Creation & Server Software Providers
pages.purpose: Covers the NewInstancePage wizard, IServerSoftwareProvider pattern, and each provider implementation: VanillaProvider, PaperProvider, FabricProvider, ForgeProvider, BedrockBdsProvider, PocketmineProvider. Includes version loading, EULA acceptance, and Geyser provisioning during creation.

pages.title: Server Lifecycle & Process Management
pages.purpose: Deep dive into ServerLifecycleService, ServerProcessManager, ServerProcess, and ServerLaunchConfigurator. Covers the startup pipeline, state machine (Stopped/Starting/Online/Stopping/Crashed), graceful shutdown via RCON, auto-restart with exponential backoff, and JobObject process grouping.

pages.title: Server Configuration & Properties
pages.purpose: Explains InstanceMetadata, ServerConfiguration, ServerConfigurationService, ServerPropertiesParser, and how settings are read from and written to server.properties. Covers the ServerSettingsViewModel composition and all child sub-viewmodels.

pages.title: Backups & World Management
pages.purpose: Documents BackupService (safe save-lock handshake, locked-file tolerance, atomic writes), BackupSchedulerService, WorldManager, off-site replication, SafeZipExtractor, and backup restoration.

pages.title: Addons, Plugins & Mods
pages.purpose: Covers SettingsAddonsVM, PluginScanner, BedrockAddonInstaller, ModpackService, ModpackParser, IAddonManager, and the Poggit marketplace integration for PocketMine. Explains engine-specific addon handling (Java plugins, Bedrock .mcpack, PocketMine .phar).

pages.title: Dashboard & Console UI
pages.purpose: Overview of the two primary operational views: the Dashboard (instance list, metrics cards, actions) and the Server Console (log streaming, command input, AI diagnostics). Links to child pages for each.

pages.title: Dashboard
pages.purpose: Details DashboardViewModel, DashboardInstanceListVM, InstanceCardViewModel, DashboardActionsVM, DashboardMetricsVM, and DashboardPage XAML. Covers instance card rendering, live CPU/RAM metrics, start/stop/restart/delete actions, and memory preflight checks.

pages.title: Server Console
pages.purpose: Documents ServerConsolePage, the log ingestion pipeline (FlushTimer, ConcurrentQueue, LogLine colorization), command history and AutoSuggest, log filtering (regex, multi-keyword, level toggles), CrashBanner, ResourceWarningBar, and the Optimize function.

pages.title: Networking & Port Reliability Engine
pages.purpose: Overview of the networking subsystem: port preflight, probing, lease registry, recovery, and tunnel integration. Links to child pages for the Port Reliability Engine and the Playit.gg tunnel system.

pages.title: Port Reliability Engine
pages.purpose: Deep dive into the 20-file Networking feature: PortPreflightService, PortProbeService, PortLeaseRegistry, PortRecoveryService, PortFailureMessageService, PortDiagnosticsSnapshotBuilder, all enums (PortProtocol, PortIpMode, PortBindingRole, PortEngine, PortFailureCode), and the conflict overlap logic.

pages.title: Playit.gg Tunnel System
pages.purpose: Covers TunnelService, InstanceTunnelOrchestrator, PlayitAgentService, PlayitAgentProcessManager, PlayitAgentStateMachine, PlayitApiClient, TunnelPage, TunnelCreationGuidePage, TunnelLimitDialog, and how tunnel addresses are resolved and surfaced in the settings UI.

pages.title: Java Runtime Provisioning
pages.purpose: Documents the full Java runtime management subsystem: JavaProvisioningService, JavaAdoptiumClient, JavaRuntimeResolver, JavaRuntimeValidator, JavaProvisioningStatus/Stage, and how bundled JREs are downloaded, extracted, and validated per Minecraft version.

pages.title: Marketplace & Content Discovery
pages.purpose: Overview of the marketplace subsystem for discovering and installing server content. Links to child pages for Modrinth/CurseForge plugin browsing and map browsing.

pages.title: Plugin & Mod Browser (Modrinth & CurseForge)
pages.purpose: Covers ModrinthService (search, GetLatestVersionAsync, facet queries), CurseForgeService, PluginBrowserPage, and how search results are displayed and installed into instances.

pages.title: Map Browser & World Import
pages.purpose: Documents MapBrowserPage, SettingsWorldVM world file management, and how maps are downloaded and applied to server instances.

pages.title: AI Intelligence & Diagnostics
pages.purpose: Overview of the Observatory subsystem: AI session summaries, log analysis, dependency health monitoring, and support bundle generation. Links to child pages for AI features and diagnostics.

pages.title: AI Session Summaries & Log Analysis
pages.purpose: Documents AiApiClient (multi-provider: Gemini, OpenAI, Claude, Mistral, Groq), SessionSummarizationService, SessionLogPreprocessor, SummaryStorageService, LogSanitizer PII scrubbing, and the AnalyzeLogLineAsync flow in the console.

pages.title: Diagnostics, Health Monitoring & Support Bundles
pages.purpose: Covers DependencyHealthMonitor (Adoptium, Playit.gg, Modrinth API polling), DiagnosticReportingService (support bundle generation), PortDiagnosticsSnapshotBuilder, AppSettingsPage, and crash report writing.

pages.title: Testing
pages.purpose: Overview of the PocketMC.Desktop.Tests project: test infrastructure, hermetic workspaces, and the areas covered by automated tests. Links to child pages for unit and integration test patterns.

pages.title: Unit Tests — Services & Models
pages.purpose: Documents test coverage for ServerConfigurationService, InstanceManager, PluginScanner, ServerPropertiesParser, JavaRuntimeResolver, SafeZipExtractor, ServerProcessManager, ControlledNavigationStack, and path safety tests.

pages.title: Integration Tests — Port Reliability & Tunnel
pages.purpose: Covers PortReliabilityTestWorkspace, PortPreflightServiceTests, PortProbeServiceTests, PortRecoveryServiceTests, PortLeaseRegistryTests, PortDiagnosticsSnapshotBuilderTests, TunnelServiceTests, ServerLifecycleServiceTests, GeyserProvisioningServiceTests, and the PlayitAgentHarness mock.
